questions.text: Who is the author of the story 'The Fun They Had'?
questions.explanation: Isaac Asimov is the famous science fiction writer who wrote this story about the future of education.

questions.text: In which year is the story set?
questions.explanation: The story takes place on May 17, 2157, as recorded in Margie's diary.

questions.text: What did Tommy find in the attic of his house?
questions.explanation: Tommy found a 'real' book made of paper, which was a novelty in their era of digital books.

questions.text: How old is Margie?
questions.explanation: Margie is eleven years old, while Tommy is thirteen.

questions.text: What was strange about the book for Margie and Tommy?
options: The words stood still instead of moving
It was written in a secret code
questions.explanation: Unlike the moving text on their television screens, the printed words on the paper book remained static.

questions.text: Who told Margie about the time when all stories were printed on paper?
questions.explanation: Margie's grandfather once told her that when he was a little boy, his grandfather told him about paper books.

questions.text: What did Tommy call the paper book?
questions.explanation: Tommy thought paper books were a waste because you throw them away once you are done reading them.

questions.text: What was the subject Margie was failing in?
questions.explanation: The mechanical teacher had been giving her test after test in geography, and she had been doing worse and worse.

questions.text: Who came to fix Margie's mechanical teacher?
questions.explanation: The County Inspector was a round little man with a red face who was sent to repair the mechanical teacher.

questions.text: What was wrong with the mechanical teacher?
options: The geography sector was geared a little too quick
questions.explanation: The geography sector was adjusted to a level higher than Margie's age group, making the tests too difficult.

questions.text: What did Margie have to use to write her homework and tests?
questions.explanation: She had to write them out in a punch code she was taught when she was six years old.

questions.text: Where was Margie's schoolroom located?
questions.explanation: In 2157, schools are located within the students' homes, specifically right next to their bedrooms.

questions.text: According to the book, who was the teacher in the old schools?
questions.explanation: Margie was surprised to learn that teachers in the past were human beings, not machines.

questions.text: Why did Margie think the old kind of school was fun?
options: Because kids from the whole neighborhood came together
questions.explanation: She imagined all the kids laughing and shouting in the schoolyard, sitting together in the schoolroom.

questions.text: Which part of the mechanical teacher did Margie hate the most?
options: The slot for homework and test papers
questions.explanation: The slot where she had to put homework and test papers was the most disliked part.

questions.text: How many telebooks did Tommy's television screen have?
questions.explanation: Tommy remarked that their screen had a million books on it and was good for plenty more.

questions.text: Why did Margie's mother send for the County Inspector?
options: Because the mechanical teacher was malfunctioning
questions.explanation: She sent for him because Margie's performance in Geography was declining due to a fault in the machine.

questions.text: What did the County Inspector give Margie?
questions.explanation: He smiled at Margie and gave her an apple before starting work on the teacher.

questions.text: To what level did the County Inspector reset the geography sector?
questions.explanation: He slowed it down to an average ten-year level to match Margie's mental age.

questions.text: What did Tommy say about the human teachers of the past?
options: They didn't live in the house
questions.explanation: Tommy explained that they didn't live in the house; they had a special building where all kids went.

questions.text: What did Tommy find 'crinkly' about the old book?
questions.explanation: The pages were yellow and crinkly because the book was very old.

questions.text: What was the 'regular' teacher for Margie?
questions.explanation: In her time, a mechanical teacher was the normal or 'regular' way of being taught.

questions.text: Why was Margie disappointed when the County Inspector fixed the teacher?
options: She hoped he would take it away for a while
questions.explanation: She hoped they would take the teacher away altogether, as they had done with Tommy's for nearly a month.

questions.text: What had happened to Tommy's teacher once?
options: The history sector had blanked out completely
questions.explanation: The history sector had blanked out, so the teacher was taken away for a month to be repaired.

questions.text: What kind of school did the book describe?
options: Old kind of school from centuries ago
questions.explanation: The book described the schools from 'centuries ago' where children went to a common building.

questions.text: What was the reaction of Tommy when Margie asked if a man could be a teacher?
questions.explanation: Tommy screamed with laughter because Margie thought a man wasn't smart enough to be a teacher.

questions.text: How did Margie and Tommy learn their lessons?
questions.explanation: They studied individually at home using a computerized teacher on a screen.

questions.text: The mechanical teacher was on at the same time every day except:
questions.explanation: Margie's mother believed children learned better at regular hours, so she had school five days a week.

questions.text: What arithmetic lesson was Margie's teacher teaching when she was thinking about the old schools?
questions.explanation: The screen showed: 'Today’s arithmetic lesson is on the addition of proper fractions...'

questions.text: Margie's school was just next to her:
questions.explanation: The story specifies the schoolroom was right next to her bedroom.

questions.text: What does 'loftily' mean in the context of Tommy's speech?
questions.explanation: Tommy speaks loftily to show he knows more about the past than Margie.

questions.text: Who said, 'I wouldn't throw it away'?
questions.explanation: Tommy said he wouldn't throw his television screen away because it has so many books.

questions.text: What was the color of the County Inspector's face?
questions.explanation: He is described as a 'round little man with a red face'.

questions.text: Margie's grandfather's grandfather was a:
questions.explanation: The book belonged to the time when his grandfather's grandfather was a little boy.

questions.text: The theme of the story is:
options: The future of education/Technology in schools
questions.explanation: The story contrasts traditional human-centric schooling with futuristic automated home-based schooling.

questions.text: What did Margie write in her diary?
options: Today Tommy found a real book!
questions.explanation: She recorded the discovery of the paper book as a significant event.

questions.text: Why did Margie find the old teacher 'strange'?
questions.explanation: To Margie, the idea of a human being (a man) acting as a teacher was bizarre.

questions.text: What did children do at the end of the day in old schools?
questions.explanation: They all went home together at the end of the day, having learned the same things.

questions.text: What was the benefit of kids learning the same things in the past?
options: They could help one another with the homework
questions.explanation: Because they learned the same things, they could discuss and help each other.

questions.text: What was Margie doing at the end of the story?
options: Thinking about the fun they had
questions.explanation: Margie was sighing and thinking about the old schools and the fun those kids had.
